name: Create Project Memory

goal.answer: Create a comprehensive PROJECT_MEMORY.md file at `.agents/PROJECT_MEMORY.md` that summarizes the kambuja-pos codebase so future AI agents can onboard quickly.

criteria.answer:
File `.agents/PROJECT_MEMORY.md` exists and is readable.
File includes all 10 required sections: project purpose, tech stack, frontend structure, backend structure, database structure, scripts/run commands, environment variables, Khmer UI/business vocabulary, preservation rules, and current known issues.
No other files in the repository were modified or deleted.

constraints.answer: Do not modify or delete any existing source files., Follow the exact 10-section format requested by the user., Preserve all Khmer text exactly as it appears in the codebase.

assumptions.answer: The project structure and key files explored so far are representative of the current codebase.; There are no critical hidden issues that would materially change the content of the project memory.

phases.name: Write Project Memory
phases.goal: Synthesize all gathered project context into `.agents/PROJECT_MEMORY.md` with the required 10 sections.
phases.description: Based on the orient and exploration findings, write a single Markdown file covering project purpose, tech stack, frontend/backend/database structure, scripts, env vars, Khmer vocabulary, preservation rules, and known issues.

steps.description: Read remaining key files if needed (e.g., frontend source trees, docs, any issue lists).
Write `.agents/PROJECT_MEMORY.md` with all 10 required sections populated from codebase findings.
steps.summary: Builder agent successfully wrote `.agents/PROJECT_MEMORY.md` with all 10 required sections (project purpose, tech stack, frontend structure, backend structure, database structure, scripts/run commands, environment variables, Khmer UI/business vocabulary, preservation rules, and current known issues). File is 8,785 bytes, UTF-8 encoded, readable. No other files were modified.
